rulings.Case Title: LORAN SIMON VS. TODD BRABEC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 5, 2025 line 3. PLAINTIFF LORAN SIMON MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is OFF CALENDAR. This statutory motion for judgment on the pleadings was set to be made to the Court within 30 days of the original trial date. According to the Notice of Motion, this was solely a statutory motion for judgment on the pleadings with no reference to the common law in the Notice. Furthermore, parties failed to meet and confer in-person, via telephone or video conference. Plaintiff may file a non-statutory motion for judgment on the pleadings if necessary and proper and shall address specifically the purported defects in the answer. Plaintiff's general statements without referenced to any particular affirmative defenses or their elements in the points and authorities are not sufficient to carry the burden. (e.g. "Here, Defendant's Answer contains boilerplate, repetitive affirmative defenses rife with errors, and none of them are even affirmative defenses at all. Furthermore, none of Defendant's asserted defenses contain any factual allegations to support them. (See RJN, Ex. A, pp. 2-5.)" Points and Authorities 7:17-19). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW PAUL KANGAS VS. ESTATE OF CHRISTINA Y.C. CHEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 5, 2025 line 4. DEFENDANT ESTATE OF CHRISTINA Y.C. CHEN, LEAH PRICE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication is GRANTED. Moving party shifted its burden. Opposing party failed to create a triable issue of fact with competent admissible evidence. Plaintiff's late-filed opposition was considered and stricken. Plaintiff was provided with an opportunity to file a supplemental brief citing authorities re: late-filed brief. No relevant authorities cited. Plaintiff also filed declarations and affidavits along with a supplemental brief. Affidavits and declarations have not been authorized and are stricken. To the extent, Plaintiff's counsel asserts some kind of attorney fault relief, it is not properly before the Court. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDRA FARMER VS. ROEL C VILLACARLOS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Order And Order Restraining Judgment Debtor
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 5, 2025 line 15. PLAINTIFF ALEXANDRA FARMER Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Order And Order Restraining Judgment Debtor is DENIED without prejudice. Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. According to the documents of which judicial notice is granted and Plaintiff's argument, a Roel C. Villacarlos is to receive $67,187.50 as distribution out of the estate. This distribution is subject to three notices of assignment by Inheritance Funding Company and Probate Advance, LLC for a total amount of $109,800. Accounting for possible rebated based on repayment structure (and no evidence that any repayments have been made to date), the existing assignments are currently no less than $92,700, but may increase overtime to the full amount of $109,800. The Court also notes that Plaintiff provides no evidence that judgment debtor in this case and Roel C. Villacarlos in case PES-23-306272 are the same person. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
